content: [T001] 2018 Presidential & Parliamentary Elections — Erdoğan Wins First Round: As a Moderate Nationalist (İYİ), I react to 2018 Presidential & Parliamentary Elections — Erdoğan Wins First Round through my existing identity, media exposure, and lived pressures.

[T002] Turkish Lira Currency Crisis — US-Turkey Brunson Sanctions: As a Moderate Nationalist (İYİ), I react to Turkish Lira Currency Crisis — US-Turkey Brunson Sanctions through my existing identity, media exposure, and lived pressures.

[T003] ECHR Orders Demirtaş Release — Turkey Refuses: As a Moderate Nationalist (İYİ), I react to ECHR Orders Demirtaş Release — Turkey Refuses through my existing identity, media exposure, and lived pressures.

[T030B_first_round_result_revealed] ROUND1: First Round — Erdoğan 49.52%, Kılıçdaroğlu 44.88%, Oğan 5.17%: As a Moderate Nationalist (İYİ), I react to ROUND1: First Round — Erdoğan 49.52%, Kılıçdaroğlu 44.88%, Oğan 5.17% through my existing identity, media exposure, and lived pressures.

[T033] RUN-C: Kılıçdaroğlu Signs Özdağ Protocol — '13 Million Refugees' Pledge: As a Moderate Nationalist (İYİ), I react to RUN-C: Kılıçdaroğlu Signs Özdağ Protocol — '13 Million Refugees' Pledge through my existing identity, media exposure, and lived pressures.

[T035B_final_result_revealed] FINAL: Presidential Runoff — Erdoğan Wins 52.18% vs Kılıçdaroğlu 47.82%: As a Moderate Nationalist (İYİ), I react to FINAL: Presidential Runoff — Erdoğan Wins 52.18% vs Kılıçdaroğlu 47.82% through my existing identity, media exposure, and lived pressures.

[T001] 2018 Presidential & Parliamentary Elections — Erdoğan Wins First Round: I'm feeling a mix of anger and sadness after Erdoğan's win. The concentration of power in the new presidential system worries me, and I fear for the future of our democracy.

[T002] Turkish Lira Currency Crisis — US-Turkey Brunson Sanctions: I'm feeling a mix of anger and fear due to the currency crisis and the government's handling of the situation. The economic turmoil is affecting my daily life and I worry about the future of our country.

[T003] ECHR Orders Demirtaş Release — Turkey Refuses: I feel frustrated and saddened by the refusal of the Turkish government to comply with the ECHR ruling on Demirtaş. This situation reflects a troubling disregard for the rule of law and further erodes my trust in our institutions.

[T004] March 2019 Local Elections — AKP Loses Ankara and Istanbul: The recent local elections have given me a glimmer of hope that change is possible, especially with the AKP losing key cities. However, I still feel frustrated and saddened by the government's refusal to comply with the ECHR ruling on Demirtaş.

[T005] YSK Annuls Istanbul Election — Rerun Ordered, İmamoğlu Wins by Landslide: I feel a mix of hope and frustration after İmamoğlu's victory, as it shows that change is possible, but my trust in institutions like the YSK has taken a hit. The political landscape feels increasingly polarized, and I'm concerned about the future of democracy in Turkey.

[T006] Emine Bulut Femicide — '#OlmekIstemiyorum' Goes Viral: The tragic death of Emine Bulut has left me feeling a deep sense of anger and sadness. It's heartbreaking to see how women are not being adequately protected, and this situation makes me question the government's commitment to safety and justice.

[T007] COVID-19 Pandemic — Partial Lockdowns, Economic Shock: The COVID-19 pandemic is deeply concerning, and I feel a mix of anger and sadness about how the government is handling the situation. The economic impact is hitting everyone hard, and I worry about the future of our country.

[T008] Turkey Withdraws from Istanbul Convention on Women's Rights: I am deeply troubled by the government's decision to withdraw from the Istanbul Convention. It feels like a step backward for women's rights and safety in Turkey, and I worry about the implications for the future.

[T009] HDP Closure Case Filed — Party Under Existential Legal Threat: I'm feeling a mix of anger and sadness about the government's actions against the HDP. It seems like a dangerous precedent for democracy and the rights of all political parties in Turkey.

[T010] Lira Free-Fall — 60% Devaluation in 2021 After Central Bank Rate Cuts: I'm feeling frustrated and anxious about the economic situation in Turkey. The devaluation of the lira is impacting everyone, and I worry about how this will affect my family's financial stability.

[T011] Parliament Rejects Pension Hike to Minimum Wage — Pensioner Anger: I'm deeply frustrated by the government's decision to reject the pension hike. This feels like a betrayal to those who have worked hard their entire lives and now struggle to make ends meet.

[T012] Inflation Peaks at 80%+ — Worst Cost-of-Living Crisis in 24 Years: I'm feeling increasingly frustrated and angry about the government's handling of the economy, especially with inflation soaring and the rejection of the pension hike. It's hard to see a future where things improve for my family and community.

[T001] 2018 Presidential & Parliamentary Elections — Erdoğan Wins First Round: I'm feeling a mix of anger and sadness after Erdoğan's win. The concentration of power in the new presidential system worries me, and I fear for the future of our democracy.

[T002] Turkish Lira Currency Crisis — US-Turkey Brunson Sanctions: I'm feeling a mix of anger and fear due to the currency crisis and the government's handling of the situation. The economic turmoil is affecting my daily life and I worry about the future of our country.

[T003] ECHR Orders Demirtaş Release — Turkey Refuses: I feel frustrated and saddened by the refusal of the Turkish government to comply with the ECHR ruling on Demirtaş. This situation reflects a troubling disregard for the rule of law and further erodes my trust in our institutions.

[T004] March 2019 Local Elections — AKP Loses Ankara and Istanbul: The recent local elections have given me a glimmer of hope that change is possible, especially with the AKP losing key cities. However, I still feel frustrated and saddened by the government's refusal to comply with the ECHR ruling on Demirtaş.

[T005] YSK Annuls Istanbul Election — Rerun Ordered, İmamoğlu Wins by Landslide: I feel a mix of hope and frustration after İmamoğlu's victory, as it shows that change is possible, but my trust in institutions like the YSK has taken a hit. The political landscape feels increasingly polarized, and I'm concerned about the future of democracy in Turkey.

[T006] Emine Bulut Femicide — '#OlmekIstemiyorum' Goes Viral: The tragic death of Emine Bulut has left me feeling a deep sense of anger and sadness. It's heartbreaking to see how women are not being adequately protected, and this situation makes me question the government's commitment to safety and justice.

[T007] COVID-19 Pandemic — Partial Lockdowns, Economic Shock: The COVID-19 pandemic is deeply concerning, and I feel a mix of anger and sadness about how the government is handling the situation. The economic impact is hitting everyone hard, and I worry about the future of our country.

[T008] Turkey Withdraws from Istanbul Convention on Women's Rights: I am deeply troubled by the government's decision to withdraw from the Istanbul Convention. It feels like a step backward for women's rights and safety in Turkey, and I worry about the implications for the future.

[T009] HDP Closure Case Filed — Party Under Existential Legal Threat: I'm feeling a mix of anger and sadness about the government's actions against the HDP. It seems like a dangerous precedent for democracy and the rights of all political parties in Turkey.

[T010] Lira Free-Fall — 60% Devaluation in 2021 After Central Bank Rate Cuts: I'm feeling frustrated and anxious about the economic situation in Turkey. The devaluation of the lira is impacting everyone, and I worry about how this will affect my family's financial stability.

[T011] Parliament Rejects Pension Hike to Minimum Wage — Pensioner Anger: I'm deeply frustrated by the government's decision to reject the pension hike. This feels like a betrayal to those who have worked hard their entire lives and now struggle to make ends meet.

[T012] Inflation Peaks at 80%+ — Worst Cost-of-Living Crisis in 24 Years: I'm feeling increasingly frustrated and angry about the government's handling of the economy, especially with inflation soaring and the rejection of the pension hike. It's hard to see a future where things improve for my family and community.

[T013] İmamoğlu Sentenced to 2.5 Years — Opposition Rallies Around Him: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration over İmamoğlu's sentencing. It seems like the government is trying to silence opposition voices, and it's disheartening to see how political maneuvering affects our democracy.

[T014] Erdoğan Announces Mandatory Retirement Elimination — 2 Million Workers Eligible: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration regarding the government's recent economic measures. While the removal of the mandatory retirement age might benefit some, it feels like a desperate attempt to win votes rather than a genuine solution to our economic crisis.

[T015] Constitutional Court Freezes HDP Treasury Aid: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration over the Constitutional Court's decision to freeze HDP's treasury aid. It seems like a politically motivated move to weaken the opposition right before the elections, which is deeply concerning for our democracy.

[T016] EQ-A: Kahramanmaraş Earthquake Day — 7.8 and 7.7 Magnitude Double Strike: I am deeply shaken by the recent earthquake and the devastation it has caused. My thoughts are with those affected, and I feel a growing anger towards the government's response and lack of accountability for past failures in building regulations.

[T017] EQ-B: AFAD Response Chaos — Rescue Teams Delayed, Deaths Mount: I'm feeling overwhelmed by the government's failure to respond effectively to the earthquake. The rising death toll and the chaos surrounding rescue efforts have left me angry and disillusioned with the leadership.

[T018] EQ-C: Twitter/TikTok Banned During Earthquake Rescue — Public Outcry: I'm feeling increasingly frustrated with the government's response to the earthquake, especially after the ban on Twitter and TikTok during a critical time for rescue efforts. It's hard to maintain hope when accountability seems lacking.

[T019] EQ-D: Erdoğan Admits Response Problems, Visits Disaster Zone: I feel a mix of anger and disappointment after Erdoğan's admission of problems in the earthquake response. While I appreciate the acknowledgment, the ongoing chaos and the ban on social media during the rescue efforts have left me feeling hopeless about the government's ability to manage this crisis effectively.

[T020] EQ-E: Contractors Arrested — Construction Amnesty and Building Safety Scandal: I'm feeling a deep sense of anger and frustration after the recent revelations about the contractors' arrests linked to the building safety scandal. It's disheartening to see how government policies contributed to such a tragic loss of life, and I worry about the lack of accountability.

[T021] EQ-F: Opposition Mayors Respond — CHP Aid vs. Government Aid Narrative Battle: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration as I see opposition mayors stepping up to provide aid while the central government struggles. It's disheartening to witness this narrative emerge, highlighting the government's failures in response to the disaster.

[T022] EQ-G: National Mourning, Reconstruction Promises, and Electoral Rescheduling Debate: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration as I see the government's inadequate response to the earthquake. The opposition's actions highlight the failures of the current administration, making me question their ability to lead effectively.

[T023] OPP-A: Akşener Leaves Table of Six — Opposition Crisis: I'm feeling increasingly frustrated and concerned about the opposition's unity as Akşener leaves the Table of Six. This situation makes me question whether we can effectively challenge Erdoğan in the upcoming elections.

[T024] OPP-B: Opposition Reunites — Kılıçdaroğlu Nominated with İmamoğlu/Yavaş VP Formula: I'm relieved that Akşener has returned to the Table of Six, and I'm hopeful that Kılıçdaroğlu's nomination can unite the opposition. However, I still have concerns about the effectiveness of this alliance against Erdoğan.

[T025] Constitutional Court Lifts HDP Treasury Aid Freeze: I'm relieved that the Constitutional Court has lifted the HDP's treasury aid freeze, which may help the opposition, but I remain concerned about the ongoing closure case. The unity of the opposition is crucial, and while I'm hopeful, I still feel the weight of political fatigue.

[T026] Erdoğan Announces Pension Increase to 7,500 Lira — Pre-Election Largesse: I'm feeling frustrated with Erdoğan's pension increase announcement, as it seems like a tactic to buy votes rather than address the real issues facing retirees. The gap between pensions and inflation is still concerning, and I worry about the effectiveness of the opposition in countering such strategies.

[T027] HDP/YSP Formally Endorses Kılıçdaroğlu — Kurdish Vote Behind Opposition: I'm feeling conflicted about the HDP's endorsement of Kılıçdaroğlu. While it could energize the opposition, it also raises concerns about how nationalists will perceive this alliance. The political landscape feels increasingly tense, and I'm worried about the implications for our unity.

[T028] Erdoğan Raises Civil Servant Salaries 45% — Five Days Before Election: I'm feeling frustrated with Erdoğan's last-minute salary increase for civil servants, as it seems like a desperate attempt to buy votes rather than a genuine solution to the economic issues we face. The political climate is tense, especially with the HDP's endorsement of Kılıçdaroğlu, which complicates my feelings about the opposition's unity.

[T029] CAND-A: Muharrem İnce Withdraws — Citing Smear Campaign / Deepfake Video: I'm feeling frustrated and concerned about the political landscape, especially with İnce's withdrawal and the smear campaign against him. It feels like the opposition is consolidating, but I'm still wary of how this will affect our unity as nationalists.

[T030A_first_round_vote_decision] First-round presidential vote decision: I'm feeling a mix of frustration and concern about the political situation, especially with İnce's withdrawal. I still believe in the İYİ Party and want to support Kılıçdaroğlu in the first round, but I'm worried about the unity among nationalists.

[T030B_first_round_result_revealed] ROUND1: First Round — Erdoğan 49.52%, Kılıçdaroğlu 44.88%, Oğan 5.17%: I'm feeling frustrated and concerned about the political landscape after the first round results. The fact that Erdoğan is so close to winning outright is alarming, and I'm worried about how this will affect the unity among nationalists moving forward.

[T031] RUN-A: Kılıçdaroğlu's Nationalist Turn — Anti-Refugee Rhetoric Intensifies: I'm feeling increasingly frustrated and anxious about the political landscape, especially with Kılıçdaroğlu's shift toward anti-refugee rhetoric. It complicates my support for him as I worry about the implications for national unity and the future of our country.

[T032] RUN-B: Sinan Oğan Endorses Erdoğan: I'm feeling increasingly frustrated with the political landscape, especially with Oğan's endorsement of Erdoğan. It's disheartening to see the divisions among nationalists and the implications for Kılıçdaroğlu's chances.

[T033] RUN-C: Kılıçdaroğlu Signs Özdağ Protocol — '13 Million Refugees' Pledge: I feel increasingly conflicted about Kılıçdaroğlu's recent nationalist shift, especially with the Özdağ Protocol. While I still support the İYİ Party, I worry that this approach may alienate important voter groups and ultimately hurt our chances against Erdoğan.

[T034] RUN-D: Kurdish Voter Reluctance — HDP Maintains Support Despite Protocol: I'm feeling increasingly conflicted about Kılıçdaroğlu's recent nationalist shift and the Özdağ Protocol. While I still support the İYİ Party, I'm worried about the impact this may have on our unity and the potential abstention of Kurdish voters.

[T035A_runoff_vote_decision] Runoff presidential vote decision: I'm feeling torn about the runoff decision. While I want to support Kılıçdaroğlu, I worry about the nationalist rhetoric and how it might alienate some voters. It's frustrating to see the political landscape shift so dramatically.

[T035B_final_result_revealed] FINAL: Presidential Runoff — Erdoğan Wins 52.18% vs Kılıçdaroğlu 47.82%: I'm feeling a mix of anger and sadness after the runoff results. It's disheartening to see Erdoğan win again, especially after the hopes I had for change with Kılıçdaroğlu. The political landscape feels more divided than ever, and I worry about our future.
